title: Marc Rowan on Private Markets, Software Repricing, and Capital Allocation
content: Marc Rowan, Apollo Global Management's cofounder and CEO, discusses building Apollo from Drexel's collapse into a top alternative asset manager managing $6 billion within a year. He covers private credit's rise, private markets' growing role in financing the real economy, and intersections between venture-backed technology and large-scale private financing including AI, data centers, and robotics. Rowan emphasizes that enduring organizations must adapt rather than protect the status quo.

title: 稳住基本盘的小米，紧追DeepSeek
content: Xiaomi reported resilient Q1 results with 99.1 billion yuan revenue despite surging memory costs, as its "human-vehicle-home" ecosystem diversified revenue: auto contributed 25% of gross profit versus phones' 20%. The company is aggressively pursuing AI, cutting MiMo model API prices by up to 99% to compete with DeepSeek, while committing 60 billion yuan to AI over three years. Xiaomi delivered 80,856 vehicles in Q1, expanded IoT globally, and advanced its agentic AI assistant miclaw across devices. The firm is executing an 8 billion HKD share buyback and planning 20 billion HKD more, signaling long-term confidence amid short-term cost pressures.

title: 讲讲我的中转站建站历程，大家似乎都不太关注中转站实际使用的模型，最近我的 gpt-oss-120b（别名成 GPT-5.5）蜜罐被盗刷炸了
content: The author built a zero-cost API relay using Render, Docker, and GitHub to aggregate hundreds of AI models, sharing base URL and key with friends. They created honeypots by aliasing weak models (gpt-oss-120b/20b) as "GPT-5.5/GPT-4.5." The "GPT-5.5" honeypot was discovered and heavily abused, exhausting Render's 200GB monthly traffic limit and causing automatic shutdown. Logs captured usage but were lost during redeployment. The author plans to migrate to cheaper VPS options (Dedirock, Oracle Cloud) and implement fine-grained auditing via Bifrost for better control.
eventKey: 讲讲我的中转站建站历程-大家似乎都不太关注中转站实际使用的模型-最近我的-gpt-oss-120b-别名成-gpt-5

title: Last Week in AI #341 - Musk loses to OpenAI, Google's IO updates, OpenAI solves Erdős
content: A federal jury rejected Elon Musk's $150 billion lawsuit against OpenAI, clearing the way for its potential trillion-dollar IPO. Google unveiled major AI updates at I/O 2026 including Gemini Spark, a 24/7 personal agent, and Gemini Omni, a multimodal video generator. OpenAI and Google DeepMind both made breakthroughs on 80-year-old Erdős math problems using different approaches. The Take It Down Act enforcing deepfake removal took effect, while SpaceX filed for a massive IPO and plans to acquire Cursor despite xAI's struggles. Pope Leo XIV issued the Church's first comprehensive AI encyclical warning against unchecked development.
source: Last Week in AI - Last Week in AI
eventKey: last-week-341-musk-loses-openai
normalizedTitle: last week in ai 341 - musk loses to openai google s io updates openai solves erdős
representativeSources: Last Week in AI - Last Week in AI

title: 字节跳动向 Seed 员工开放「豆包股」认购权
content: ByteDance introduced "Doubao shares," stock options tied to its Seed AI division, priced at $13 per share. The Q4 2025 incentive plan ties future valuation to the Doubao and enterprise AI business to retain talent. Core Seed engineers previously received extra monthly options worth up to about 130,000 yuan vesting over 18 months.
